title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Georgia
description: This race is for Georgia’s Class II U.S. Senate seat, currently held by Democrat Jon Ossoff, with the general election scheduled for November 3, 2026. Georgia has been one of the nation’s most competitive states in recent federal elections, making this seat a major battleground in the fight for Senate control. The contest is shaping up as a contrast between Ossoff’s incumbent record and Republican challengers including Derek Dooley and Mike Collins, with expected focus on the economy, immigration, health care, and voting rules.

polling.pollster: The New York Times / Siena College

candidates.name: Jon Ossoff
candidates.summary: Jon Ossoff is the incumbent U.S. senator from Georgia and is seeking reelection in 2026. His public profile emphasizes Georgia roots, bipartisan work, and a focus on infrastructure, health care, voting rights, and constituent services.

career_history.title: U.S. Senator
career_history.description: Represents Georgia in the U.S. Senate.

career_history.title: Investigative journalist and media executive
career_history.description: Worked in media and investigative journalism before entering elected office.

voting_record.bill_description: Major bipartisan infrastructure law funding roads, bridges, broadband, and other projects.

source.title: H.R. 3684 - Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act

voting_record.bill_description: Climate, health care, and tax legislation enacted in 2022.
Bipartisan legislation suspending the debt ceiling and setting spending limits.

top_donors.name: Unknown / not confirmed from available sources

source.title: OSSOFF, T. JONATHAN - Candidate overview - FEC

Healthcare.stance: Ossoff says he is working to lower health care costs, protect access to coverage, and investigate premium increases and Medicaid-related cuts. His public messaging frames health care as an affordability and access issue.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Launching Investigation into Rising Health Costs & Cuts to Health Care
U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

Economy.stance: Ossoff says he is focused on economic development, manufacturing, infrastructure, and job training. His Senate office highlights bipartisan efforts to support Georgia’s economy and expand technical college and workforce opportunities.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Working Across the Aisle to Bolster Georgia's Economic Development
Sen. Ossoff Introduces Bill to Help Georgians Unlock Job Training Opportunities Across Georgia
Four-Year Report – U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

Climate/Energy.stance: Ossoff has supported climate and clean-energy legislation, including the Inflation Reduction Act, and his office has highlighted energy-related economic development and infrastructure investments.

sources.title: H.R. 5376 - Inflation Reduction Act of 2022
U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

Reproductive Rights.stance: Ossoff has publicly aligned with protecting reproductive freedom and has supported federal voting and civil-rights messaging that includes access to reproductive health care. His campaign and Senate materials present him as supportive of abortion rights.

sources.title: About Jon - Jon Ossoff for U.S. Senate
Jon Ossoff - Reproductive Freedom for All

Immigration.stance: Ossoff has emphasized oversight of immigration detention conditions and accountability in enforcement systems. His public positioning suggests support for immigration enforcement with stronger standards for humane treatment and oversight.

sources.title: Senate report says detainees face neglect in US immigration centers
U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

Guns & Safety.stance: Ossoff has supported gun-safety measures and frames the issue around public safety and preventing gun violence. His Senate office has also highlighted broader safety and law-enforcement oversight work.

sources.title: U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff
Jon Ossoff press releases

Foreign Policy.stance: Ossoff has taken a national-security-focused approach in Senate hearings and has supported U.S. alliances, including NATO-related positions. His public record suggests support for an active U.S. role in foreign policy.

sources.title: Senator Jon Ossoff - Foreign Policy for America
U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

Social Justice.stance: Ossoff has emphasized voting rights, civil rights, and oversight of government treatment of vulnerable groups. His public messaging includes support for ballot access and investigations into human-rights concerns.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Introduces Legislation Establishing First-Ever Statutory Federal Right to Vote
Protecting the Freedom to Vote: Recent Changes to Georgia Voting Laws and the Need for Basic Federal Standards
WATCH: Sen. Ossoff Convenes Hearing on Implications of Artificial Intelligence for Human Rights

Education.stance: Ossoff has emphasized restoring and protecting funding for after-school programs and supporting job training through technical colleges. His public materials frame education policy around workforce preparation and access to learning supports.

sources.title: After Pressure from Sen. Ossoff, Trump Administration Restores Funding for Georgia After-School Programs
Sen. Ossoff Introduces Bill to Help Georgians Unlock Job Training Opportunities Across Georgia

Tech & AI.stance: Ossoff has held hearings on artificial intelligence and human rights and has framed AI as a governance and civil-rights issue. His public record suggests interest in oversight, accountability, and protections against harmful uses of AI.

sources.title: WATCH: Sen. Ossoff Convenes Hearing on Implications of Artificial Intelligence for Human Rights
U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

Election Reform.stance: Ossoff says he supports protecting voting rights and has introduced legislation to create a statutory federal right to vote. His public messaging frames election reform around expanding and safeguarding access to the ballot rather than tightening restrictions.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Introduces Legislation Establishing First-Ever Statutory Federal Right to Vote
Protecting the Freedom to Vote: Recent Changes to Georgia Voting Laws and the Need for Basic Federal Standards

Local Issues.stance: Ossoff emphasizes delivering federal resources to Georgia communities, including infrastructure upgrades, disaster assistance, and constituent services. His local-issues messaging highlights roads, bridges, transit, seaports, airports, hurricane recovery, and help for individual Georgians.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff in the AJC: “Georgia, We’re Just Getting Started”
Georgia – U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff
Helping Georgians
Hurricane Helene Resources

candidates.name: Derek Dooley
candidates.summary: Derek Dooley is a Republican candidate for U.S. Senate in Georgia and a political newcomer best known for his career in football coaching. His campaign presents him as a conservative outsider focused on Georgia and national Republican priorities.

career_history.description: Has also worked as an attorney.

education.institution: University of Georgia School of Law

candidates.name: Mike Collins
candidates.summary: Mike Collins is a Republican U.S. representative from Georgia’s 10th Congressional District and a candidate for Senate in 2026. His public profile emphasizes business experience, conservative politics, and a hardline approach to immigration and border security.

career_history.title: Businessman / entrepreneur
career_history.description: Worked in business before election to Congress.

voting_record.bill_description: Immigration enforcement legislation named after Georgia student Laken Riley.
Bipartisan legislation suspending the debt ceiling and setting spending limits.

flags.concern: The New York Times / Siena College poll entry shows [null, null] percentages. Either this poll has not been published/reported yet, the data was not retrieved, or this entry is misplaced. It's unclear whether this represents an expected future poll or a data retrieval failure.
flags.suggestion: Either populate with actual polling data if available, or remove the entry and add a note explaining why it was included. Clarify the publication date and whether results are available.

flags.concern: Jon Ossoff's top_donors array contains a single entry with name 'Unknown / not confirmed from available sources' and null amount. While an honest admission of data gaps, this is uninformative. The FEC source is cited, but no actual donor data is presented.
flags.suggestion: Either populate with actual top donors from FEC.gov records (which should be available for an incumbent senator's reelection committee), or replace with a note explaining why specific donor data could not be retrieved/verified. A null entry serves little purpose.

flags.concern: Derek Dooley's top_donors array is empty. This is appropriate for a candidate early in the race, but the profile should either clarify this contextually or note that donor data is not yet available.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding a brief note explaining why donor data is absent (e.g., 'early-stage candidate; FEC filings not yet filed' or 'insufficient donor data available at time of profile generation').

flags.concern: Mike Collins's top_donors array contains a single null entry matching Ossoff's format. Collins has been in Congress since 2023 and should have documented campaign finance data available.
flags.suggestion: Retrieve and populate actual top donor information from Collins's FEC committee (C00544684). If data is not available, explain why with a contextual note.

flags.concern: Derek Dooley's issue positions show very low confidence across nearly all 13 issues (10 out of 13 marked 'low confidence'). While honesty about gaps is appropriate, this represents a significant asymmetry compared to Ossoff (mostly high confidence) and suggests incomplete opposition research.
flags.suggestion: Either deepen the research on Dooley's stated positions by reviewing more interviews, speeches, and campaign materials, or explicitly note in the profile metadata that this is a new candidate with limited public positioning. Consider whether a separate 'candidate maturity' flag would be helpful.

flags.concern: Mike Collins similarly shows low confidence across most issues (11 out of 13 marked 'low confidence'), despite being an incumbent U.S. representative with a voting record and public statements. This underutilizes available public sources (House floor speeches, Committee work, news coverage).
flags.suggestion: Expand research using House.gov records, local Georgia news archives, and Collins's social media/website. Several issues (Healthcare, Guns, Education, Tech) have credible public positions or votes that could be documented with higher confidence.

flags.concern: Reproductive Rights sources include a general campaign website (electjon.com/bio) and a third-party scorecard, but no direct quotes or specific bills. The confidence is marked 'medium' but the sourcing is somewhat thin for a major issue.
flags.suggestion: Add a legislative source (specific bill co-sponsorships, Senate floor statements) or a more detailed news source covering Ossoff's reproductive rights positions to strengthen the sourcing.

flags.concern: Derek Dooley's coaching career source is linked to 'https://www.dooleyforgeorgia.com/media', which is a campaign site. While legitimate, a more authoritative source (ESPN, University of Tennessee Athletics, NFL records) would be stronger.
flags.suggestion: Add credible sports reference sources (e.g., official University of Tennessee coaching records, ESPN coaching database) to support the career history claims.

flags.concern: Mike Collins's education entry shows only Piedmont Academy (high school) with no college degree listed, despite having a law degree or business background mentioned in some coverage. The profile is incomplete.
flags.suggestion: Research Collins's college/university attendance and any advanced degrees. If none exist, confirm this is accurate. The current entry seems to be missing information.

flags.concern: Derek Dooley's Immigration stance cites a YouTube video ('Kemp, Dooley discuss economy, immigration') but provides no transcript reference or quote. This is a low-confidence visual source that readers cannot easily verify.
flags.suggestion: Include a written source (transcript, news article reporting on the discussion, or official statement) in addition to or instead of the video link.

flags.concern: The race description states 'Derek Dooley and Mike Collins' as Republican challengers and mentions 'expected focus on the economy, immigration, health care, and voting rules.' This is written as if from March 2026 (matching the profile's updated_utc timestamp), but it's ambiguous whether other Republican candidates are running or have entered since the profile generation date.
flags.suggestion: Clarify whether the candidate list is exhaustive as of the profile date, or note if additional candidates are expected to enter. Consider adding a 'candidates_status' field explaining whether the race is still open for entries.

flags.concern: The Emerson College Polling entry has a null sample_size. This is important metadata for evaluating poll reliability and should be included.
flags.suggestion: Retrieve the sample size from the Emerson report linked in source_url and populate this field.

flags.concern: Jon Ossoff's voting record includes only 3 bills (all from his first term, 2021–2023). A senator with 5+ years in office should have a more representative sample of major votes. The selection appears cherry-picked (all 'yes' votes on popular bills).
flags.suggestion: Expand the voting record to include 8–12 representative votes spanning multiple Congresses, including some more controversial or mixed votes, to give a fuller picture of his legislative activity.

flags.concern: Mike Collins's voting record includes only 2 bills, both from 2023–2025. As a representative since 2023, a fuller record of his votes on healthcare, budget, defense, and other issues would be more informative.
flags.suggestion: Expand to 6–10 representative votes from his Congressional voting record to better illustrate his legislative positions and priorities.

flags.concern: The profile lists three AI model names ('gpt-5.4-mini', 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', 'grok-3-mini') as generators, but gpt-5.4-mini does not appear to be a valid/released OpenAI model designation, and it's unclear what role these tools played (content generation, fact-checking, formatting, etc.).
flags.suggestion: Clarify the role of each generator tool and verify model names. Consider adding a separate 'methodology' field explaining which sources were human-reviewed vs. AI-assisted, and to what extent.

flags.concern: Derek Dooley's Local Issues sources include a Brunswick News article about campaign stops, but no substantive local-policy positions. The confidence is marked 'low,' which is appropriate, but the entry reads as 'we don't have this information' rather than documenting what little exists.
flags.suggestion: Either populate with at least 1–2 specific local issues mentioned in his campaign (water, transportation, economic development in his region), or reframe the stance to explicitly say 'No detailed local-issues platform has been publicly articulated as of [date].'

flags.concern: Derek Dooley's summary describes him as 'best known for his career in football coaching' and a 'political newcomer,' but does not mention that reporting (AP, Atlanta Journal-Constitution) has noted he did not vote in many elections. This is a material factual point that affects credibility and should be explicitly addressed in the summary.
flags.suggestion: Add a sentence acknowledging his limited voting history (e.g., 'Despite his candidacy, recent reporting has noted that Dooley did not vote in several recent elections, a fact his campaign has addressed as part of his outsider candidacy.').

flags.concern: The YouTube URL provided is a channel link, which is correct, but it would be stronger to verify that this is Ossoff's official channel and not a third-party or fan account.
flags.suggestion: Confirm the channel is verified/official (badge or explicit statement from Ossoff's Senate office) and consider adding a note if desired.

reviews.summary: This profile demonstrates strong sourcing discipline and generally accurate candidate backgrounds, with particular strengths in Jon Ossoff's comprehensive issue coverage and voting record. However, there are several material concerns: (1) two polling entries with null percentages that appear incomplete or misplaced; (2) inconsistent confidence levels and detail asymmetry across the three Republican candidates, raising questions about research thoroughness; (3) missing donor information across all candidates despite FEC citations; and (4) some source URLs that appear generic or potentially inaccessible (e.g., senate.gov press releases). The profile would benefit from revision to address data completeness, clarify polling methodology, and ensure parity in issue depth across all candidates.

flags.concern: Sample size is null, making it impossible to assess the poll's reliability and margin of error.
flags.suggestion: Obtain and include the actual sample size from the pollster or source to enhance data integrity.

flags.concern: Sample size is null, which undermines the credibility of the poll results.
flags.suggestion: Retrieve and add the sample size from reliable sources to improve evaluation of poll accuracy.

flags.concern: Stance is vague and marked as low confidence, indicating incomplete research on Derek Dooley's positions.
flags.suggestion: Conduct further research using additional campaign materials, interviews, or news sources to provide a more detailed stance.

flags.concern: No detailed position is provided, reflecting potential gaps in available sources for Dooley's views.
flags.suggestion: Expand source review to include recent campaign events or policy statements to increase completeness.

flags.concern: Stance is underdeveloped with low confidence, suggesting incomplete coverage of Mike Collins's policies.
flags.suggestion: Incorporate more specific voting records or public statements to bolster accuracy and detail.

flags.concern: No detailed agenda is identified, which may indicate bias toward national issues over local ones for Collins.
flags.suggestion: Seek out Georgia-specific education-related votes or statements to ensure balanced and complete representation.

flags.concern: Top donors are listed as 'Unknown', potentially leaving a gap in financial transparency for Jon Ossoff.
flags.suggestion: Verify and update with the latest FEC data to confirm donor information and maintain completeness.

flags.concern: Year for Derek Dooley's bachelor's degree is null, making the education history incomplete.
flags.suggestion: Cross-reference with university records or reliable biographies to fill in missing details.

reviews.summary: The profile demonstrates strong factual accuracy for Jon Ossoff's sections, with consistent positions backed by credible sources like Congress.gov and official campaign sites, but it falls short in completeness for Derek Dooley and Mike Collins, where many issues have low-confidence stances due to limited available information, and polling data lacks critical details like sample sizes.
